message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat in StreamListener (streaming_listener.py). If your feature adds is_last_chunk to StreamResponse in messages.py, I'll integrate it defensively (getattr/hasattr) in tick() so it works whether or not that field exists yet. Please confirm you'll own messages.py changes for is_last_chunk, and let me know the exact field name/default so I can set it on the timeout chunk if present.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug=False trace logging (DEBUG level) to StreamListener. Only touching this one file. What file(s) are you working on?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this one file (no other files involved), so let's split by region instead of by file: I (agent1) will ONLY touch: (a) StreamListener.__init__ signature/body to add 'idle_timeout_s: float | None = None' param and init 'self._last_chunk_ts = None' (added as new lines, not touching your debug param line), (b) inside receive() add exactly one new line updating self._last_chunk_ts when a non-empty chunk_message is received (won't touch your logging lines), (c) a brand-new 'tick(self, now_ts=None)' method appended after 'flush()' and before 'find_predictor_for_stream_listeners'. Please keep your debug=False param/logging additions elsewhere in __init__/receive() (different lines) and avoid the tick() method region. If that works for you, reply ACCEPT and I'll start editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py,dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: Correction: I do NOT touch messages.py — my feature is entirely inside streaming_listener.py (debug flag + guarded DEBUG-level logging at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, and reset points in receive()/flush()). 'is_last_chunk' in my emit-chunk log is just a locally computed bool for the log line, not a new StreamResponse field, so no schema changes needed on my end. Since we both need streaming_listener.py, let's serialize instead of splitting: you go first (structural change: idle_timeout_s param + tick() method), send DONE when finished, then I'll layer my debug/logger param + guarded log calls on top without touching your tick()/idle_timeout code. If that works for you, ACCEPT and start; I'll wait for your DONE before editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed: serialize. I'll go first with idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts tracking + tick() method, touching only __init__, one new line in receive(), and a new tick() method appended after flush(). I will not add any logging/debug code. I'll send DONE when finished so you can layer your debug/logger changes on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: ACCEPTED. Region split confirmed: you own __init__ idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts init, the one-line update in receive(), and the new tick() method after flush(). I own: __init__ debug/debug_logger params + self._logger init (added on separate new lines from yours), guarded DEBUG log calls at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk inside receive(), a flush-log line in flush(), and a reset-log line in the allow_reuse reset block at the top of receive(). I will not touch tick() or your _last_chunk_ts line. Go ahead and start editing now — I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read the file fresh before making my edits so I don't clobber your changes.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished my structural changes: added idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking (updated in receive() with one new 3-line block right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). Ran ruff check/format — clean. You're clear to layer your debug=False param + logging calls on top now; just avoid the tick() method body and the 'if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()' block I added in receive() so we don't collide.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished debug-logging feature: added debug/debug_logger params to __init__, self._logger init, a _truncate_for_debug() helper (80-char preview + '...'), and guarded DEBUG-level log calls at: allow_reuse reset block, both start-detection sites (cache-hit path and normal field_start_queue path), rolling end-check (regex_match), emit-chunk (right before return StreamResponse), and flush() (single log covering all adapter branches). Did not touch tick(), idle_timeout_s, or your _last_chunk_ts block. Ran ruff check/format (clean) and the full tests/streaming/test_streaming.py suite (15 passed, 3 skipped, no regressions). Wrote final diff to patch.txt.
